---
title: Editing `.qmd` files
---

A `.qmd` file is the maintained source document: readable Markdown combined
with optional YAML configuration and executable cells. Work in source
documents while a local preview is running whenever possible.

## Front matter and content

The YAML front matter at the top configures the page; normal content follows
the closing `---`:

```markdown
---
title: "Protein structure exercise"
format: html
---

## Inspect the structure

Use the downloaded model to answer the questions below.
```

Change front matter only when the update requires page configuration. Ordinary
wording, figure, or exercise updates belong in the body.

## Figures

Add a figure using a path relative to the source document:

```markdown
![Active site residues](media/active-site.png){width=65%}
```

Quarto can arrange labelled subfigures:

```markdown
::: {#fig-comparison layout-ncol=2}
![Before treatment](media/before.png){#fig-before}
![After treatment](media/after.png){#fig-after}

Comparison of measurements.
:::
```

For a small floating illustration, a project may use an HTML-styled div:

```markdown
::: {style="float: right; margin: 10px; width: 28%;"}
![Menu location](media/menu.png)
:::
```

Always preview figure paths, size, alignment, captions, and accessibility
text. Some repositories provide a figure-checking task.

## Callouts and reusable content

Use ordinary Quarto callouts for hints or warnings:

```markdown
::: {.callout-tip}
Use the same units throughout the calculation.
:::
```

Quarto includes can avoid copying a long external code example:

<pre><code>```default
{{&lt; include scripts/example.pml &gt;}}
```</code></pre>

Paths remain relative to the current source document unless configured
otherwise.

## Downloads and custom features

One existing course site provides a custom download-button shortcode:

<pre><code>
{{&lt; download-button path="../files/example.pml" filename="example.pml" &gt;}}
</code></pre>

This is extension-dependent syntax, not a built-in requirement for all course
repositories. Use it only where the repository already configures the
shortcode, and run its download-link check task if available.

## Authoring loop

1. Run the repository's preview task.
2. Edit the relevant `.qmd` file and related resources.
3. Inspect every affected audience/profile and output format.
4. Render and run any configured content checks.
5. Review `git diff` before committing.
